text.title: Requirements Traceability
text.summary: RTM structure, MoSCoW invariants, and agent-specific gotchas for requirements extraction and traceability maintenance.

section.title: Traceability Matrix

rule.statement: Store the RTM as YAML or CSV in source control, not as a Word document.
rule.rationale: An RTM that lives in a Word doc becomes orphaned after sprint 2. Source-control storage means agents and humans can edit it, diffs are reviewable, and it survives tool migrations.

section.p: Every row in the RTM must carry: requirement ID, source stakeholder, design reference, code reference, test reference, and current status. A must-have requirement missing any of these is a gap.

example.code: - id: FR-01 type: functional statement: "System calculates order totals automatically on item add/remove" priority: must source: "Sales Manager (interview 2026-04-01)" acceptance_criteria: "Order total updates within 200ms with no page reload" design_ref: "design/checkout-flow.md#totals" code_ref: "src/cart/calculator.py" test_ref: "tests/test_cart.py::test_total_update" status: done
example.note: UUIDs or content-hash IDs prevent renumbering when requirements are regenerated by agents.

section.title: MoSCoW Rules

rule.statement: Apply a quota: no more than 60% of requirements may be "Must".
rule.rationale: Without a cap, stakeholders mark everything Must and scope becomes uncontrollable. A 60% Must quota forces explicit prioritization trade-offs.

list.item: Must — delivery-blocking; project fails if absent; requires AC and owner
Should — high value but not delivery-blocking; could move to phase 2
Could — nice-to-have; absorbed only if capacity permits after Must and Should
Won't — explicitly deferred or excluded; prevents "assumed in scope" disputes

rule.statement: A Must requirement must never have status "Won't".
rule.rationale: This combination means the project will fail by definition. Detect early and force a sponsor decision: descope the Must or cancel the project.

section.reference: Enforce MoSCoW invariants (no MUST without AC, no MUST+Wont)
Flag must-have requirements missing design/test coverage

rule.statement: Freeze canonical requirement text and require diff-based edits; do not regenerate from scratch.
rule.rationale: LLMs paraphrase requirements into different wording on each pass. Regenerating produces a new document with different IDs and wording — breaking all downstream traceability links.

list.item: Force "priority" as a required JSON field in extraction prompts; agents silently drop it otherwise
Tautological ACs ("system shall work correctly when it works correctly") must be rejected by the validator
Agent-extracted requirements drop source stakeholder attribution — require it as a non-nullable field
For regulated work, agents must not redact source quotes from RTM; auditors need original wording
Gold-plating: cap NFR generation to requirements with an explicit named source — reject agent-invented NFRs
